question_text: Why does the Earth experience seasons?

options.label: A
options.text: Because the Earth is closer to the Sun in summer

options.label: B
options.text: Because the Earth's axis is tilted, changing the angle of the Sun's rays

options.label: C
options.text: Because the Earth orbits the Sun faster in summer

options.label: D
options.text: Because the Sun moves around the Earth in different paths

explanation: The Earth's axis is tilted at about 23.5 degrees. As Earth orbits the Sun, this tilt means different parts of Earth receive the Sun's rays at different angles—steeper angles in summer and shallower in winter, causing seasons.
